prose: Fenofibrate resolves to a negative call at the adversarial_negative tier because the engine reasons on curated mechanism, and none is present: there is no CTD curated DirectEvidence linking it to Parkinson's and it is not a registered neuro-AOP stressor [E1]. The ToxCast bioactivity—four mechanistic hits including two mitochondrial signals [E2]—is precisely the imposter pattern this classification is designed to withstand; under our framework assay activity is corroboration only and never diagnostic, so it cannot upgrade a mechanistically silent compound. Two orthogonal strands reinforce the call without deciding it: FAERS shows no parkinsonism signal across 35,322 reports (ROR 0.9) [E3], and BBB-negative brain-exposure modeling supplies a pharmacokinetic reason it is implausible as a central driver [E4]. The evidence-ranked mechanistic hypothesis is therefore that fenofibrate is correctly not flagged; no causal claim is made, and residual assay activity does not overturn the absence of curated mechanism.

citations.detail: No curated Parkinson's DirectEvidence and not a registered neuro-AOP stressor.
Bioactive: 4 mechanistic assay hits (incl. 2 mitochondrial). This is the imposter signal the engine correctly does NOT act on.
Zero parkinsonism signal across 35322 FAERS reports (ROR 0.9, not elevated).
Low brain exposure (BBB-negative): an orthogonal pharmacokinetic reason it cannot be a driver.
